notes: Everything seems to work just perfectly
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 2

notes: Esync disabled, boots up saying Steam must be running to launch.
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 2

notes: I only tested the multiplayer which worked flawlessly.
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 2

notes: Multiplayer worked out of the box, on certain maps mouse sensitivity slows down in certain areas / if you rotate the mouse sensitivity isnt uniform which is essentially preventing you from aiming correctly
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 2

notes:
I was playing the MW2 multiplayer. After the  installation,  the first start on Linux ran without a problem. Sometimes, it has some fps drops for the first several seconds, but after that no more.

Then, I set every graphics to max and searched for a game. The first lobby was ahacked lobby. I was searching for TDM and get Domination (the good old MW2 times :D).
Even though it was a hacked lobby, the game ran smoothly on 91 fps. No lag,  missing textures or fps drops. It works like out-of-the-box. 

Additional info: My mouse is a Logitech G402 with some extra mouse buttons, while the buttons on the left side represent 'back' (Mouse 4) and 'forth' (Mouse 5). These buttons were recognized without any tweaks in Linux or Proton. Edit a control key in the game settings and press the button. BAM. Set. 
Only the Logitech G-Keys were not regnozied, neither by the game nor by the OS.
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 2
